Here’s the list of players going on tour:

Goalkeepers: Berni, Handanovic, Padelli;

Defenders: Bastoni, D'Ambrosio, Dalbert, De Vrij, Ranocchia, Skriniar, Ntube, Pirola;

Midfielders: Agoumé, Barella, Borja Valero, Brozovic, Candreva, Gagliardini, Joao Mario, Lazaro, Nainggolan, Perisic, Sensi;

Forwards: Longo, Politano, Colidio, Esposito, Vergani.

In the coming days, Godin, Vecino and Puscas will join the rest of the group.

George Puscas has declared his undying love for Inter by telling reporters he would ‘walk to the other side of the earth’ for the Nerazzurri.

The striker has been included in Antonio Conte’s squad for the Nerazzurri’s preseason tour in Asia, with the first team flying out to Singapore earlier today.

Puscas completed a series of medical tests in Rome this morning and will take part to ensure Conte is not too short of numbers up front.

“Inter are the club to whom I owe everything,” the 23-year-old told Gazeta Sporturilor.

“It’s an honour for me to wear my club’s shirt again.”

Palermo were supposed to sign Puscas permanently last month after signing him on loan for the 2018-19 season.

The Rosanero went bankrupt this summer, however, meaning they defaulted on their payment.Inter will face Manchester United, Juventus and Paris Saint-Germain on tour before they return to Milan on 28 July.

Let’s get to know our new player Nicolo Barella!

Nicolo is a hard working midfielder, with an eye for tackling. Nicolo is 22 years old, and 172 cm tall(5’8.)
He was born in Cagliari, the same place Inter signed him from. Nicolo has also played for Italy, all the way from the u-15, to the “Real deal”.

Nicolo has 2 children and his wife with him aswell, and we hope they all settle in very well!🖤💙

West Ham are growing increasingly confident as the game of ‘swings and roundabouts ‘ continues over their big money move for Eintracht Frankfurt’s Sebastien Haller.

Despite stories of overnight interest from a Champion League club there is still no firm evidence evidence of that being the case and certainly no definite names in the frame at this stage.

Both Inter Milan and Dortmund have been mentioned but CandH has learned from Italian media sources that the Serie A outfit are focused entirely on Manchester United’s Romelu Lukaku and a Hammers insider told us on the ‘phone: “It isn’t Dortmund.”

Meanwhile, the player has been at Rush Green this afternoon taking in the training facilities where he was spotted by several members of the Hammers staff.

We understand that he has now completed his medical but there are still issues to be ironed out between the club and the agent.

The hitches which have arisen may well have been at that level which is not unusual in the latter stages of a deal but within the club, insiders are becoming confident again that they have their man with the next 24 hours likely to prove crucial.

Antonio Conte warned there was little room for encouragement after Inter lost 1-0 against Manchester United today.
Mason Greenwood’s second-half strike condemned the Nerazzurri to defeat in their opening International Champions Cup match, amid sweltering conditions in Singapore.
Conte praised his side’s work-rate but admitted Inter had a lot of work still to do.
“The responses we’re getting are not positive,” he told Corriere dello Sport.
“It’s important to see the lads so committed in a difficult period like this, when you have a lot of intense training sessions.
“We have a lot of room for improvement when we’re pressing and in possession, but it’s hard to put those things into practice after so few days working.
“We have an important base to work from, which is the spirit these guys showed. We have very limited time to prepare for the next match though, and at the moment we need to train as well as play games so we can improve.”
Conte tried playing Ivan Perisic at left wing-back in his 3-5-2 system but the Italian was not convinced by his performance.
“I don’t think Ivan is suited to the position where I want him to play,” he admitted.
“On the other hand I thought Dalbert played with great application.
“We knew we would be struggling in this match and that preseason in general would be difficult for various reasons. We shouldn’t forget how hot it was out there today, which influenced the high press we had prepared.
“There’s a lot of work to do and we’ll do it.”
